overview.zh: 本周大佬圈两种情绪并行：一边是模型厂商之间的火药味——Sam Altman 连发三条夹枪带棒的推文，OpenAI 应用负责人预告 800 万活跃用户里程碑；另一边是 builder 们把智能体推进真实生产的兴奋——云端维护智能体已经开始「打架」，自动调实验的 agent 成为新积木。最硬的数据信号来自 Guillermo Rauch：开源权重模型的网关 token 占比从 4 月的 11% 飙到 29%。同时「绅士科学家时代回归」——用 AI 改电子书固件、边开车边语音写应用，个人黑客文艺复兴正在发生。
overview.en: Two moods ran in parallel among builders this week: model-vendor friction — Sam Altman fired off three pointed tweets while OpenAI's app lead teased an 8M-active-user milestone — and the thrill of pushing agents into real production, where cloud maintainer agents are already 'fighting' and experiment-tuning agents became a new building block. The hardest data point came from Guillermo Rauch: open-weight models now serve 29% of gateway tokens, up from 11% in April. Meanwhile the 'Gentleman Scientist era' is back — AI-hacked e-reader firmware and voice-coding apps while driving mark a personal-hacker renaissance.

title.zh: 模型厂商暗战与里程碑

summary.zh: Sam Altman 本周格外活跃：一边罕见承认「看到我们的模型终于擅长设计，还是有点难以置信」，一边连发两条暗讽竞品的推文（包括那条「我以为是讽刺号，一直在找 c1audeai 的拼写」）。OpenAI 应用负责人 Thibault 则预告 800 万活跃用户庆祝日并发布 ChatGPT Work。头部厂商的竞争已经从发布会打到了时间线上。
summary.en: Sam Altman had an unusually loud week: a rare admission that 'it still sorta breaks my brain to see our models be good at design finally,' plus two pointed jabs at competitors (including the 'kept looking for the handle to be spelled c1audeai' one). OpenAI's app lead Thibault teased an 8M-active-user celebration and ChatGPT Work. Frontier-lab competition has moved from launch events to the timeline.

quotes.author: Sam Altman
quotes.text: still sorta breaks my brain to see our models be good at design finally

quotes.author: Sam Altman
quotes.text: i thought this was satire, kept looking for the handle to be spelled c1audeai or something

quotes.author: Thibault Sottiaux
quotes.text: Tomorrow might be 8M active user celebration day. Just saying

summary.zh: 智能体不再是 demo：Peter Steinberger 把维护者智能体搬上云端（「它们已经开始打架了」）；Vercel 给智能体开放了 feature flag 实验能力，让网站自我优化；Aaron Levie 给出企业落地模板——前沿模型当管理者、低成本模型干粗活。Swyx 晒出的「大项目工作栈」则展示了多模型分工（规划/评审/编码各司其职）已是资深玩家的日常。
summary.en: Agents are past the demo stage: Peter Steinberger moved maintainer agents to the cloud ('they are fighting already'); Vercel gave agents the power to set up and tune feature-flag experiments toward self-optimizing sites; Aaron Levie sketched the enterprise template — frontier intelligence as manager, cheaper models as workhorses. Swyx's 'Big Boy projects' stack shows multi-model division of labor is now routine for power users.

quotes.author: Peter Steinberger
quotes.text: I moved our maintainer agent to the cloud and they are fighting already.

quotes.author: Guillermo Rauch
quotes.text: Powerful building block for autonomous, self-optimizing websites and applications. Give agents the ability to set up and tune experiments with feature flags.

quotes.author: Aaron Levie
quotes.text: Here's a great post on driving down costs, while maintaining high performance, with frontier intelligence as a manager and lower cost models for the workhorse tasks. This will be the template.

quotes.author: Swyx
quotes.text: where i'm currently at for Big Boy projects: sol ultra to plan / fable 5 to critique / sonnet 5, terra ultra, swe 1.7 to ultracode…

summary.zh: Garry Tan 一句「绅士科学家时代回归了」（1000+ 赞）点破本周最浪漫的趋势：Ryo Lu 用 Cursor 给电子书阅读器写了带中日文竖排排版的自制固件；Nikunj Kothari 边开车边用语音提示词一把梭出报销自动化工具并开源；Amjad Masad 在训练「个人模型」时感叹这就是早期氛围编程的感觉。AI 把硬件黑客和个人软件的门槛打到了地板上。
summary.en: Garry Tan's 'Era of the Gentleman Scientist is so back' (1,000+ likes) named the week's most romantic trend: Ryo Lu shipped custom e-reader firmware with beautiful CJK vertical typography using Cursor; Nikunj Kothari one-shotted an expense-automation tool by voice prompt while driving, then open-sourced it; Amjad Masad said training personal models 'feels like early vibe coding.' AI has floored the barrier to hardware hacking and personal software.

quotes.author: Garry Tan
quotes.text: Era of the Gentleman Scientist is so back

quotes.author: Ryo Lu
quotes.text: built a custom e-reader firmware with Cursor, hardware hacking is fun! made for: beautiful latin + CJK typography, vertical layout, line breaking, large character sets (縱書．禁則)…

quotes.author: Nikunj Kothari
quotes.text: Btw this was one-shot by fable with a voice prompt while driving on the way to SF with some steering and edits obviously

quotes.author: Amjad Masad
quotes.text: Getting realtime progress updates on my model training runs. This feels like early vibe coding except it's making personal models.

summary.zh: 本周最值得存档的一条数据：Guillermo Rauch 披露开源权重模型在其网关的 token 占比从 4 月的 11% 涨到 29%。结合 Levie 对「前沿模型指挥+廉价模型干活」成本结构的判断，闭源模型的定价压力正在从舆论走向账本——这与本周周报里 GLM 5.2 毛利坍塌的讨论互为印证。
summary.en: The week's most archivable data point: Guillermo Rauch disclosed that open-weight models' share of gateway tokens jumped from 11% in April to 29%. Read alongside Levie's manager/workhorse cost-structure thesis, pricing pressure on closed models is moving from discourse to ledgers — corroborating this week's GLM 5.2 margin-collapse debate in the radar.

quotes.author: Guillermo Rauch
quotes.text: "Open-weight models ran 29% of gateway tokens, up from 11% in April"

quotes.author: Aaron Levie
quotes.text: A few thoughts on what we will see in AI structurally for the foreseeable future: Frontier intelligence continues unabated and pushes the industry forward continuously…

summary.zh: 与本周周报「AI 反噬经济」主线呼应：Peter Yang 公开征讨那些秒回大号的 AI 机器人账号（「每周都要拉黑一批」）；Zara Zhang 分享「不靠 slop 涨粉」的创作心法。当 AI 内容泛滥成为默认状态，「人味」本身正在成为稀缺资产。
summary.en: Echoing this week's 'AI backlash economy' storyline in the radar: Peter Yang called out AI bot accounts that instantly reply to big accounts ('I block a bunch every week'), and Zara Zhang shared how she grows an audience 'without the slop.' As AI content floods becomes the default, humanness itself is turning into the scarce asset.

quotes.author: Peter Yang
quotes.text: is it possible to find accounts that just instantly reply to posts from bigger accounts? Those are usually AI bots worth banning? I block a bunch every week.

quotes.author: Zara Zhang
quotes.text: Back in March I recorded a 45-min conversation with @ashebytes on building in public, growing my audience on X (without the slop), and my thoughts on vibe coding
